content: The 2024-2025 generation of state-of-the-art active learning agent memories can be reconfigured to rank novel environment states by their "perceived memorability gap"—the mismatch between expected encoding strength and actual retrieval failure—rather than just prediction error, and this gap can be used as a more stable intrinsic reward for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning than existing ICM or RND methods. This replaces the noisy prediction error reward common to ICM and RND with a metric that already emerges naturally from modern active memory systems' built-in uncertainty tracking.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
